subject: English
chapter: The Case of the Fifth Word
source_pdf: The Case of the Fifth Word.pdf

key_points: Encyclopedia Brown, also called Leroy Brown, is known for his exceptional memory and logical reasoning which help him solve mysteries that even the police find difficult
Chief Brown, his father and the Chief of Police, often discusses unsolved cases with him during dinner, trusting his analytical abilities
Tim Nolan and Daniel Davenport were suspected of committing a jewellery robbery, but the stolen jewellery was never discovered
Before Nolan died, he left a coded message with four strange words written on a calendar sheet as a clue for Davenport
Encyclopedia Brown decoded the pattern of the words and realized that they represented the days of the week, leading to the discovery of the hidden jewellery
examples: The coded words written by Nolan were Nom, Utes, Sweden, and Hurts
These words were formed by removing the word 'day' from the days Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday
The missing fifth word represented Friday
Encyclopedia connected Friday with the 'fir' tree mentioned in Nolan’s nursery
The jewellery was eventually found hidden inside a container of soil beneath the young fir tree
formulas_or_processes: Situation → Nolan dies leaving behind a mysterious coded message
Clue Identification → Four unusual words written on a calendar sheet
Pattern Recognition → Words represent days of the week without the letters 'day'
Logical Deduction → The missing fifth day is Friday, hinting at a fir tree
Conclusion → The stolen jewellery is hidden under the fir tree in Nolan’s nursery
exam_tips: Focus on how Encyclopedia Brown applies observation, pattern recognition, and logical deduction to solve the case
Understand the importance of the calendar sheet as a contextual clue for decoding the message
Remember the coding method used by Nolan—removing the word 'day' from the days of the week
Explain clearly how the missing fifth word leads to the discovery of the hiding place
